antipattern.description: UUID set as the primary key directly (`id = UUIDField(primary_key=True, ...)`).
antipattern.reason: Joins slow down ~2x on large tables, indexes balloon ~25%. The right pattern is integer PK + separate UUID field exposed publicly.

antipattern.description: API URLs expose the integer PK: `/api/v1/orders/12345/`.
antipattern.reason: Enumerable IDs leak scale ("competitor sees we processed 12k orders") and enable IDOR-style attacks.

antipattern.description: BaseModel without `Meta.abstract = True`; every read incurs an implicit JOIN.
antipattern.reason: Performance tax on every query for no semantic benefit.
Django 2+ requires explicit on_delete; implicit was removed because the default was unsafe and silently cascaded.

antipattern.description: Composite query pattern with no Meta.indexes covering it.
antipattern.reason: FK auto-index covers the FK column alone; multi-column filters do a full scan.
